calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-06-2024 Vacated

MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Compel Compliance With March 14, 2023 Order And For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff [C.C.P. 2023.030(D)]; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities
Off Calendar Jun-22-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. KEVIN CARDONA, Esq. of Millstein Fellner LLP (Tel: 415-348-0348) for defendant Marina Security Services, Inc.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MARINA SECURITY SERVICES, INC.'s Motion To Compel Compliance With March 14, 2023 Order And For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff [C.C.P. 2023.030(D)].

The Court's tentative ruling instructed parties to appear.

The Court confirms it is has now received from defense counsel the missing Exhibit 7 to the moving party's declaration.

The Court rules as follows: Defendant's motion for terminating sanctions is granted. Plaintiff's case is dismissed with prejudice.

The pro tem judge will email to defense counsel the complete verbiage to be put into a proposed order regarding today's ruling. Defense counsel is to thereafter prepare and submit a proposed order in Word format to the pro tem judge at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Off Calendar Mar-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MARINA SECURITY SERVICES, INC.'S Motion To Compel Compliance With October 26 2022 Court Order And Mpa

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH OCTOBER 26 2022 COURT ORDER AND MPA
Off Calendar Mar-14-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. KEVIN CARDONA, Esq. of Millstein Fellner LLP (Tel: 415-348-0348) for defendant Marina Security Services, Inc.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MARINA SECURITY SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH OCTOBER 26 2022 COURT ORDER.

Defendant stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for defendant (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion granted. The Court orders the requested issue and evidentiary sanctions. The Court further orders Plaintiff to provide discovery responses by April 28, 2023. If no discovery responses are received by April 28, 2023, Defendant may file a motion to compel and renew its request for terminating sanctions.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Huibonhoa; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
